name: RandomTree.Uniform

unions.name: Tree
unions.comment: Type that represents binary tree with elements subjected to random picking up in uniform probabilities.

values.name: count
values.comment: Returns how many elements the tree has.

values.name: filter
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which fails to passes the test function given as the first parameter.

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Returns a `Tree` that has all elements in given list.

values.name: get
values.comment:
Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `Tree` given as parameter.
The time complexity is _O(log(N))_.

values.name: insert
values.comment: Inserts a data to an already-existing `Tree`.

values.name: insertList
values.comment: Inserts all elements of given list into the tree.
values.type: List.List a -> RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a -> RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a

values.name: map
values.comment: Maps all the content of the tree.

values.name: remove
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which is the same as the first parameter from the second parameter.

values.name: replace
values.comment:
Random generator that replaces one data from the tree and generates a pair consisting of the removed data and resultant tree. For example,

    replace x  ／＼
             ／    ＼
           ／＼    ／＼
         ／＼  c  d    e
        a    b

generates

    ( a，  ／＼    ),
         ／    ＼
       ／＼    ／＼
     ／＼  c  d    e
    x    b

    ( b，  ／＼    ),
         ／    ＼
       ／＼    ／＼
     ／＼  c  d    e
    a    x

    ( c，  ／＼    ),
         ／    ＼
       ／＼    ／＼
     ／＼  x  d    e
    a    b

    ( d，  ／＼    ),
         ／    ＼
       ／＼    ／＼
     ／＼  c  x    e
    a    b

or

    ( e，  ／＼    ),
         ／    ＼
       ／＼    ／＼
     ／＼  c  d    x
    a    b

in probability of 20%. The time complexity is _O(log(N))_.
values.type: a -> RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( a, RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a )

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Returns a `Tree` that has only one element.

values.name: take
values.comment:
Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `Tree`, paired with the rest part of the tree. For example,

    take
            ／＼
          ／    ＼
        ／＼    ／＼
      ／＼  c  d    e
     a    b

generates

    ( a， Just  ／＼    ),
              ／    ＼
            ／＼    ／＼
           b    c  d    e

    ( b，Just   ／＼    ),
              ／    ＼
            ／＼    ／＼
           a    c  d    e

    ( c，Just  ／＼    ),
             ／    ＼
          ／＼     ／＼
          a    b  d    e

    ( d，Just   ／＼    ),
              ／    ＼
            ／＼    ／＼
           a    b  c    e

or

    ( e，Just   ／＼    )
              ／    ＼
            ／＼    ／＼
           a    b  c    d

in probability of 20%. The time complexity is _O(log(N))_.
values.type: RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( a, Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a) )

name: RandomTree.Weighted

unions.name: Tree
unions.comment: `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree` represents non-empty collection of randomly chosen values.

values.name: adjustTotalWeightTo
values.comment: Adjusts the total weight of the tree to the specified value.

values.name: drop
values.comment: Returns `Random.Generator` that generates the argument tree whose `n` elements are removed randomly. The returned value is wrapped in `Maybe` just in case all the elements have been removed.

values.name: filter
values.comment: Filters the `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree` by removing elements that do not satisfy the provided predicate function. The result is wrapped in a `Maybe` type to indicate if the tree is empty after filtering.
values.type: (( Basics.Float, a ) -> Basics.Bool) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a)

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Creates a `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree` from a single element and a list of additional elements. This ensures that the tree is non-empty.
values.type: ( Basics.Float, a ) -> List.List ( Basics.Float, a ) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: get
values.comment: Returns a `Random.Generator` that produces a tuple containing a random content from the tree and its associated weight.

values.name: getWeight
values.comment: Returns the weight of the specified `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree`.

values.name: insert
values.comment: Inserts a weighted element into the `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree`, adjusting the structure as needed.
values.type: ( Basics.Float, a ) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: insertList
values.comment: Inserts all weighted elements from the provided list into the `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree`.
values.type: List.List ( Basics.Float, a ) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: map
values.comment: Maps the weighted contents of the tree using the provided function.
values.type: (( Basics.Float, a ) -> ( Basics.Float, a )) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: mapContent
values.comment: Maps the content of each element in the tree using the provided function.
values.type: (( Basics.Float, a ) -> a) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: mapWeight
values.comment: Maps the weight of each element in the tree using the provided function.
values.type: (( Basics.Float, a ) -> Basics.Float) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: merge
values.comment: Merges two `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree` instances into a single tree, preserving weights of each elements.
values.type: RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: pick
values.comment: Returns a `Random.Generator` that produces a tuple containing a randomly selected weighted content from the tree and a `Maybe` value representing the modified tree with that content removed.
values.type: RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( ( Basics.Float, a ), Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a) )

values.name: remove
values.comment: Removes all instances of the specified element from the `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree`. The result is wrapped in a `Maybe` type to indicate if the tree is empty after removal.

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Creates `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree` from a single element and its associated weight.

values.name: take
values.comment: Returns a `Random.Generator` that produces n weighted contents selected randomly from the tree. The result includes the remaining tree structure.
values.type: Basics.Int -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( List.List ( Basics.Float, a ), Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a) )

values.name: toList
values.comment: Converts a `RandomTree.Weighted.Tree` into a list of tuples containing the weights and their corresponding contents.

name: RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted

unions.name: Tree
unions.comment: Type that represents binary tree with weighted elements subjected to random picking up.

aliases.name: Weighted
aliases.comment:
Type alias that represents each weighted contents in `Tree`. The probability of being chosen is proportional to its weight.
The weight is represented with `WideFloat.WideFloat` type in `kudzu-forest/elm-wide-float` package.

aliases.name: WideFloat
aliases.comment:
Type that represent floating point number with wider range than `Float` in core package. This is introduced for preventing overflow of exponentially changing value.
Please hit `elm install kudzu-forest/elm-wide-float` in your terminal.

values.name: adjustContent
values.comment: Maps content in `Element`.

values.name: adjustWeight
values.comment: Returns weighted datum whose weight is multiplied with the given `Float`.

values.name: count
values.comment: Returns how many elements the tree has. The time complexity is _O(1)_

values.name: delete
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which is the same as the first parameter from the second parameter. The returned value is wrapped in `Maybe`. The time complexity is _O(N)_.

values.name: filter
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which passes the test function given as the first parameter from the second parameter. The returned value is wrapped in `Maybe`.
values.type: (RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.WideFloat -> a -> Basics.Bool) -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a -> Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a)

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Returns a `Tree` that has all element in given list.Never give this function a negetive-or-zero-weighted element.(If the weight is negative or zero, the weight is automatically converted to 1.)
values.type: RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Weighted a -> List.List (RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Weighted a) -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a

values.name: fromPairs
values.comment: Creates `Tree` from list of tuples of a `Float` value corresponding to its weight as the first component, and a datum as the second component. Never give this function a negetive-or-zero-weighted element.(If the weight is negative or zero, the weight is automatically converted to 1.)
values.type: ( Basics.Float, a ) -> List.List ( Basics.Float, a ) -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a

values.name: get
values.comment: Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `Tree` given as parameter. The probability for each elements to be chosen is proportional to its weight. The time complexity is _O(log(N))_.

values.name: insert
values.comment: Inserts a Weighted data to an already-existing `Tree`. Never give this function a negetive-or-zero-weighted element.(If the weight is negative or zero, the weight is automatically converted to 1.)
values.type: RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Weighted a -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a

values.name: insertListWithRelativeWeight
values.comment: Inserts all elements of given list into the tree of second parameter. The first components of the tuple denotes the relative weight(`1` means the whole weight of the original tree).
values.type: List.List ( Basics.Float, a ) -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a

values.name: insertWithRelativeWeight
values.comment: Inserts an element into the tree. The first parameter denotes the relative weight(`1` means the whole weight of the original tree). The time complexity is _O(log(N))_.
values.type: Basics.Float -> a -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a

values.name: map
values.comment: Maps all the content of the tree, with the weights unchanged.

values.name: replace
values.comment: Random generator that replaces one weighted data from the tree and generates a pair consisting of the removed data and resultant tree. The time complexity is _O(log(N))_
values.type: RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Weighted a -> RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Weighted a, RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a )

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Returns a `Tree` that has only one element. Never give this function a negetive-or-zero-weighted element.(If the weight is negative or zero, the weight is automatically converted to 1.)

values.name: take
values.comment:
Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `Tree`, paired with the rest part of the tree.
If the `Tree` has only one element, then `Nothing` is returned as the second component.
The time complexity is _O(log(N))_.
values.type: RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Weighted a, Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.WideFloatWeighted.Tree a) )

values.name: totalWeight
values.comment: Returns the sum of weight of all the elements in the tree. The time complexity is _O(1)_
